rangeFormattingRules.description: Format Angular decorator metadata with consistent indentation, placing each property on its own line with 2-space indent inside the decorator argument object
Ensure consistent indentation of class members including properties, methods, and accessors with 2-space indent from the class body brace
Align multi-line template literal content preserving relative indentation within inline component templates
Sort import statements alphabetically within groups: Angular core, Angular modules, third-party libraries, and local imports separated by blank lines
Format constructor dependency injection parameters one per line with consistent indentation and access modifier alignment
Format array and object literals with consistent bracket placement, trailing commas, and indentation for providers, imports, and declarations arrays
Align chained method calls such as RxJS pipe operators and builder patterns with each call on a new indented line
Format Angular control flow blocks (@if, @for, @switch, @defer) with consistent indentation and brace placement
